channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: ARC-AGI-2 Technical Report
item.description: arXiv:2603.06590v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC) is designed to assess generalization beyond pattern matching, requiring models to infer symbolic rules from very few examples. In this work, we present a transformer-based system that advances ARC performance...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, demonstration, example, model, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hierarchical Latent Structures in Data Generation Process Unify Mechanistic Phenomena across Scale
item.description: arXiv:2603.06592v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Contemporary studies have uncovered many puzzling phenomena in the neural information processing of Transformer-based language models. Building a robust, unified understanding of these phenomena requires disassembling a model within the scope of its t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, model, augmented, LLM, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hierarchical Embedding Fusion for Retrieval-Augmented Code Generation
item.description: arXiv:2603.06593v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-augmented code generation often conditions the decoder on large retrieved code snippets. This ties online inference cost to repository size and introduces noise from long contexts. We present Hierarchical Embedding Fusion (HEF), a two-stage ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, model, augmented, vector, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Rethinking Personalization in Large Language Models at the Token Level
item.description: arXiv:2603.06595v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With large language models (LLMs) now performing strongly across diverse tasks, there is growing demand for them to personalize outputs for individual users. Personalization is typically framed as an additional layer on top of a base NLP task, requiri...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, LLM, large language model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: "Dark Triad" Model Organisms of Misalignment: Narrow Fine-Tuning Mirrors Human Antisocial Behavior
item.description: arXiv:2603.06816v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The alignment problem refers to concerns regarding powerful intelligences, ensuring compatibility with human preferences and values as capabilities increase. Current large language models (LLMs) show misaligned behaviors, such as strategic deception, ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, model, fine-tuning, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Validation of a Small Language Model for DSM-5 Substance Category Classification in Child Welfare Records
item.description: arXiv:2603.06836v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Background: Recent studies have demonstrated that large language models (LLMs) can perform binary classification tasks on child welfare narratives, detecting the presence or absence of constructs such as substance-related problems, domestic violence, ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, large language model, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MedInjection-FR: Exploring the Role of Native, Synthetic, and Translated Data in Biomedical Instruction Tuning
item.description: arXiv:2603.06905v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Instruction tuning has become essential for adapting large language models (LLMs) to follow domain-specific prompts. Yet, in specialized fields such as medicine, the scarcity of high-quality French instruction data limits effective supervision. To add...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, vision, model, LLM, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Language Shapes Mental Health Evaluations in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.06910v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study investigates whether large language models (LLMs) exhibit cross-linguistic differences in mental health evaluations. Focusing on Chinese and English, we examine two widely used models, GPT-4o and Qwen3, to assess whether prompt language sys...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, model, LLM, large language model, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Scaling Strategy, Not Compute: A Stand-Alone, Open-Source StarCraft II Benchmark for Accessible Reinforcement Learning Research
item.description: arXiv:2603.06608v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The research community lacks a middle ground between StarCraft IIs full game and its mini-games. The full-games sprawling state-action space renders reward signals sparse and noisy, but in mini-games simple agents saturate performance. This complexity...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, experiment, arxiv, research, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Best-of-Tails: Bridging Optimism and Pessimism in Inference-Time Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2603.06797v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Inference-time alignment effectively steers large language models (LLMs) by generating multiple candidates from a reference model and selecting among them with an imperfect reward model. However, current strategies face a fundamental dilemma: ``optimi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, model, LLM, large language model, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Symmetry-Constrained Language-Guided Program Synthesis for Discovering Governing Equations from Noisy and Partial Observations
item.description: arXiv:2603.06869v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Discovering compact governing equations from experimental observations is one of the defining objectives of quantitative science, yet practical discovery pipelines routinely fail when measurements are noisy, relevant state variables are unobserved, or...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> product, framework, RAG, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LEAD: Breaking the No-Recovery Bottleneck in Long-Horizon Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.06870v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-horizon execution in Large Language Models (LLMs) remains unstable even when high-level strategies are provided. Evaluating on controlled algorithmic puzzles, we demonstrate that while decomposition is essential for stability, extreme decompositi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, large language model, arxiv, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LieCraft: A Multi-Agent Framework for Evaluating Deceptive Capabilities in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.06874v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit impressive general-purpose capabilities but also introduce serious safety risks, particularly the potential for deception as models acquire increased agency and human oversight diminishes. In this work, we present ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, model, LLM, large language model, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: vLLM Hook v0: A Plug-in for Programming Model Internals on vLLM
item.description: arXiv:2603.06588v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern artificial intelligence (AI) models are deployed on inference engines to optimize runtime efficiency and resource allocation, particularly for transformer-based large language models (LLMs). The vLLM project is a major open-source library to su...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, RAG, model, example, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How Attention Sinks Emerge in Large Language Models: An Interpretability Perspective
item.description: arXiv:2603.06591v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) often allocate disproportionate attention to specific tokens, a phenomenon commonly referred to as the attention sink. While such sinks are generally considered detrimental, prior studies have identified a notable exceptio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, arxiv, large language model, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Switchable Activation Networks
item.description: arXiv:2603.06601v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep neural networks, and more recently large-scale generative models such as large language models (LLMs) and large vision-action models (LVAs), achieve remarkable performance across diverse domains, yet their prohibitive computational cost hinders d...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, vision, model, LLM, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Khatri-Rao Clustering for Data Summarization
item.description: arXiv:2603.06602v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As datasets continue to grow in size and complexity, finding succinct yet accurate data summaries poses a key challenge. Centroid-based clustering, a widely adopted approach to address this challenge, finds informative summaries of datasets in terms o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, RAG, study, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Know When You're Wrong: Aligning Confidence with Correctness for LLM Error Detection
item.description: arXiv:2603.06604v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in critical decision-making systems, the lack of reliable methods to measure their uncertainty presents a fundamental trustworthiness risk. We introduce a normalized confidence score based on o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, framework, RAG, model, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Structure-Aware Set Transformers: Temporal and Variable-Type Attention Biases for Asynchronous Clinical Time Series
item.description: arXiv:2603.06605v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electronic health records (EHR) are irregular, asynchronous multivariate time series. As time-series foundation models increasingly tokenize events rather than discretizing time, the input layout becomes a key design choice. Grids expose time$\times$v...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, transformer, attention, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LegoNet: Memory Footprint Reduction Through Block Weight Clustering
item.description: arXiv:2603.06606v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As the need for neural network-based applications to become more accurate and powerful grows, so too does their size and memory footprint. With embedded devices, whose cache and RAM are limited, this growth hinders their ability to leverage state-of-t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, model, fine-tuning, memory, compression | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, context window, prompt, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, CoT, audio, chain-of-thought, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, retrieval, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> knowledge base, retrieval, framework, RAG, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, framework, LLM, vector, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: MultiGen: Level-Design for Editable Multiplayer Worlds in Diffusion Game Engines
item.description: arXiv:2603.06679v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Video world models have shown immense promise for interactive simulation and entertainment, but current systems still struggle with two important aspects of interactivity: user control over the environment for reproducible, editable experiences, and s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, memory, arxiv, context, context window | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Breaking the Martingale Curse: Multi-Agent Debate via Asymmetric Cognitive Potential Energy
item.description: arXiv:2603.06801v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-Agent Debate (MAD) has emerged as a promising paradigm for enhancing large language model reasoning. However, recent work reveals a limitation:standard MAD cannot improve belief correctness beyond majority voting; we refer to this as the Marting...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, model, large language model, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FuzzingRL: Reinforcement Fuzz-Testing for Revealing VLM Failures
item.description: arXiv:2603.06600v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision Language Models (VLMs) are prone to errors, and identifying where these errors occur is critical for ensuring the reliability and safety of AI systems. In this paper, we propose an approach that automatically generates questions designed to del...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, vision, example, model, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Scale Dependent Data Duplication
item.description: arXiv:2603.06603v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Data duplication during pretraining can degrade generalization and lead to memorization, motivating aggressive deduplication pipelines. However, at web scale, it is unclear what constitutes a ``duplicate'': beyond surface-form matches, semantically eq...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, API, embedding, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: A Coin Flip for Safety: LLM Judges Fail to Reliably Measure Adversarial Robustness
item.description: arXiv:2603.06594v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automated \enquote{LLM-as-a-Judge} frameworks have become the de facto standard for scalable evaluation across natural language processing. For instance, in safety evaluation, these judges are relied upon to evaluate harmfulness in order to benchmark ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, framework, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Counting on Consensus: Selecting the Right Inter-annotator Agreement Metric for NLP Annotation and Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2603.06865v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human annotation remains the foundation of reliable and interpretable data in Natural Language Processing (NLP). As annotation and evaluation tasks continue to expand, from categorical labelling to segmentation, subjective judgment, and continuous rat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>

item.title: Making AI Evaluation Deployment Relevant Through Context Specification
item.description: arXiv:2603.06811v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With many organizations struggling to gain value from AI deployments, pressure to evaluate AI in an informed manner has intensified. Status quo AI evaluation approaches mask the operational realities that ultimately determine deployment success, makin...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, context, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (Hugging Face Blog)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> step-by-step, example, prompt, prompt engineering, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)
